stimulus: Over the course of the 1900s, more and more Welsh children in Wales spoke only English instead of the Welsh language. To preserve the language, a group of parents and teachers founded the first Cylch Meithrin (Welsh-medium playgroup) in the 1970s. Teachers spoke only Welsh while leading activities, and the children quickly began understanding and speaking Welsh themselves. The playgroups helped the Welsh language thrive again in places where it had nearly disappeared.
stem: Which statement, if true, would most directly support the underlined claim?

answerOptions.content: Welsh shares historical roots with other Celtic languages, including Breton and Cornish.
The earliest Cylch Meithrin groups in the 1970s usually met in church halls and community centers.
Many adults in Wales today learn Welsh through evening classes after work.
In one northern Welsh town where almost no children spoke Welsh in 1970, more than 70% of children there can now speak Welsh after attending Cylch Meithrin.

rationale:
Choice D is correct because it directly shows the language thriving again in a place where it had nearly disappeared (almost none to over 70% of children).

Choice A is incorrect: language family relationships don’t show revival. Choice B is incorrect: meeting locations don’t show revival. Choice C is incorrect: adult evening classes are unrelated to the playgroups described in the underlined claim.
